channel.title: summarizedStoriesBreakingOffshootNL
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from summarizedStoriesBreakingOffshootNL, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: The ‘Make America Healthy Again’ Movement Is Cooling on Trump and Republicans
item.description: Vaccine skeptics, ‘organic moms’ and environmental activists came together to elect President Trump. But some voters are disillusioned and might not t...

item.title: Trump administration eases rules on some marijuana categories. Here's what to know
item.description: Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche said he is immediately moving medical marijuana from Schedule I to Schedule III, which includes drugs like ketami...

item.title: Airlines in Europe slash thousands of flights as Iran war cuts jet fuel supplies
item.description: The soaring cost of jet fuel is forcing European airlines to cancel tens of thousands of flights, while energy authorities warned of a possible jet fu...

item.title: New Gene Therapy Enables Children With a Rare Form of Deafness to Hear
item.description: The treatment, the first of its kind, was approved by the Food and Drug Administration on Thursday. “Our baby was born deaf, and now he can hear,” sai...

item.title: Can a mentalist trick Trump? Oz Pearlman will try in a room full of journalists
item.description: The White House Correspondents' Dinner will be headlined by a mentalist instead of a comedian. Oz Pearlman tells NPR he hopes to unify, delight and pu...

item.title: Author details the spy network that took on America's post-WWII Nazi groups
item.description: In The Secret War Against Hate , Steven J. Ross details the racist, anti-Semitic groups that sprang up in the latter half of the 20th century — and th...

item.title: How the Supreme Court’s Shadow Docket Upended Climate Policy
item.description: The Times unearthed memos that signaled a major shift in the court’s operations, in a decision that critics say was rushed and flawed.

item.title: Lebanese Journalist Killed in Israeli Strike Was Known for Being Intrepid
item.description: Mourners paid respects to Amal Khalil, who remained trapped under rubble for hours before emergency medics recovered her body.

item.title: Meta to Lay Off 10 Percent of Work Force in A.I. Push
item.description: The layoffs affect about 8,000 employees, with Meta also planning to close 6,000 open roles, as the company focuses on artificial intelligence.

item.title: Communication Failures Played Role in Deadly Crash at LaGuardia, N.T.S.B. Says
item.description: The report focuses on the lack of transponders in the fire trucks, which investigators suggested could have allowed an automatic warning system to ale...

item.title: Afghans Who Helped U.S. Forces Say They’re Being Pushed Back to the Taliban
item.description: Once promised a move to the United States, Afghan refugees who helped U.S. forces say they face ‘bad or worse’ options: resettlement to Congo or retur...

item.title: OpenAI Unveils Its New, More Powerful GPT-5.5 Model
item.description: The maker of ChatGPT is taking a more open approach to cybersecurity than its chief rival, Anthropic.

item.title: How mosquitoes — and malaria — helped shape the whereabouts of early humankind
item.description: A new study looks at an unexpected force that played a critical role in shaping the lives of ancient humans.

item.title: Iran War Live Updates: Trump to Host Israeli and Lebanese Officials at White House
item.description: The talks, scheduled for later today, were moved to the White House from the State Department. The fighting in Lebanon between Israel and Hezbollah, m...

item.title: Darrell Sheets, Auction Bidder Who Starred on ‘Storage Wars,’ Dies at 67
item.description: He appeared in 13 seasons of the long-running reality television show about storage-unit auctions. He later opened an antique shop in Arizona.
